family_criteria.family_definition: Universal access — mental health services are available to every person in India regardless of income, age, or family size. Care is delivered free at government facilities and via the 24x7 Tele MANAS helpline. No formal enrollment or means test is required.

covered_procedures: Free outpatient mental health consultation and assessment at government facilities
Free psychiatric medicines at PHC, CHC, and district hospital under DMHP
Continuing care and support for persons with severe mental disorders (schizophrenia, bipolar disorder, depression)
Management of common mental disorders — anxiety, depression, stress
24x7 free tele-counselling via Tele MANAS toll-free helpline 14416 / 1800-891-4416
Community outreach, awareness camps, and school/college mental health activities
Ambulance and referral services for acute psychiatric emergencies
Treatment at District Mental Health Programme (DMHP) centres in covered districts
excluded_procedures: Private rehabilitation or residential facility charges
Out-of-pocket purchase of medicines not stocked at the government facility

required_documents.name: Any Government Photo ID
required_documents.notes: Helpful for hospital registration, but mental health care and the Tele MANAS helpline are NOT denied for lack of ID. The 14416 helpline requires no documents at all.

enrollment_channels: District Mental Health Programme (DMHP) clinic at district hospital
Primary Health Centre (PHC) and Community Health Centre (CHC)
Government mental health institutes (NIMHANS, IHBAS, RINPAS, etc.)
Ayushman Bharat Health & Wellness Centre (Ayushman Arogya Mandir)
ASHA / ANM community health worker referral
enrollment_steps: Step 1: For immediate support, call the free Tele MANAS helpline 14416 (available 24x7 in multiple Indian languages) — no documents or registration needed
Step 2: For in-person care, visit your nearest PHC, CHC, district hospital, or DMHP clinic
Step 3: Register at the OPD counter (a government photo ID helps but is not mandatory)
Step 4: A medical officer or psychiatrist assesses your condition free of cost
Step 5: Receive free counselling, psycho-social support, and prescribed psychiatric medicines
Step 6: Attend follow-up visits for continuing care; severe cases are referred to higher facilities or DMHP support
Step 7: Community health workers may provide home follow-up and adherence support
description_for_embedding: The National Mental Health Programme (NMHP), launched in 1982 by the Ministry of Health & Family Welfare, ensures the availability and accessibility of minimum mental healthcare for all, especially vulnerable and underprivileged sections of society. It is implemented at the district level through the District Mental Health Programme (DMHP), now approved in over 760 districts, which integrates mental health into the general healthcare system. DMHP provides FREE outpatient services, assessment, counselling and psycho-social interventions, free psychiatric medicines, continuing care for persons with severe mental disorders, de-addiction support, suicide prevention, outreach, and ambulance services at Primary Health Centres, Community Health Centres, and district hospitals. There is no income test, age bar, or formal enrollment — services are available to every person in India. The National Tele Mental Health Programme (Tele MANAS), launched in October 2022, is the digital arm of DMHP, offering free 24x7 tele-counselling in multiple Indian languages through the toll-free number 14416 (or 1800-891-4416), with no documents required. NMHP covers common mental disorders such as anxiety and depression as well as severe conditions like schizophrenia and bipolar disorder. Care is also available at government mental health institutes such as NIMHANS, IHBAS, and at medical college psychiatry departments. Anyone experiencing stress, anxiety, depression, suicidal thoughts, or any mental health concern can access these free services by calling 14416 or visiting a government health facility.
